retrieved_docs.title: YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure
retrieved_docs.snippet:
YC W25 Request for Startups: AI Infrastructure

Y Combinator's Winter 2025 Request for Startups highlights AI infrastructure as a top investment thesis. Key areas include: developer tools for agentic AI (debugging, testing, deployment), evaluation frameworks for LLM reliability, multi-modal data pipelines, and cost-optimization layers for large language model inference. YC notes that startups solving agent observability and grounding are especially well-positioned.

retrieved_docs.title: Composio: Tool Integration Layer for AI Agents
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Composio: Tool Integration Layer for AI Agents

Composio provides a managed integration layer that connects LLM agents to over 250 external tools and APIs (GitHub, Slack, Notion, Jira, etc.) with built-in authentication handling. It simplifies the tool-use layer of agentic pipelines, letting developers focus on agent logic rather than API plumbing. Composio integrates natively with CrewAI, LangChain, and AutoGen.

retrieved_docs.title: Tavily Blog — accelerate-ai-development-databricks-discover-govern-and-build-mcp-and-agent-bricks
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Tavily Blog — accelerate-ai-development-databricks-discover-govern-and-build-mcp-and-agent-bricks

Connect your AI agents to a growing ecosystem of governed external services via the new MCP Catalog and Databricks Marketplace
by Elise Gonzales, Nikhil Gaekwad and Harish Gaur
Data intelligence is transforming how enterprises operate, allowing organizations to build AI agents that reason over vast amounts of proprietary data. A critical component of this evolution is the ability for AI agents to connect to external services and third-party data assets to take real-world actions. This is where the Model Context Protocol (MCP) shines, acting as a standardized way for LLMs to access external tools.
However, leveraging MCP at scale presents three major enterprise challenges:
Databricks now addresses these challenges with a secure, unified, and scalable MCP experience within Agent Bricks: the MCP Catalog (Beta) for discovery and governance, an MCP in Databricks Marketplace (Public Preview) to access a growing ecosystem of external tools, and integration with the Supervisor Agent (Beta) to enable agentic orchestration.
This launch, part of Databricks’ Week of Agents, advances our vision for AI that accurately reasons on your data. By unifying governance, discovery, and real-time action

retrieved_docs.title: HuggingFace Blog — Reachymini Appstore
retrieved_docs.snippet:
HuggingFace Blog — Reachymini Appstore

Introducing the agentic robotics appstore for 10,000 Reachy Minis
Today we're shipping an agentic toolkit for Reachy Mini, our open-source desktop robot. You describe the behavior you want in plain English, and an AI agent writes, tests, ships the code to the robot and iterates with you to get it working. No SDK to learn, no robotics background needed.
If you have a Reachy Mini (or just want to play in the browser simulator), you can get started right now by asking your favorite agent something like:
Help me build a Reachy Mini app that waves and says hello when
someone walks into the room.
Use the open-source code at https://github.com/pollen-robotics/reachy_mini
and the docs at https://huggingface.co/docs/reachy_mini/index
So far, the community has shipped over 200 apps, built by 150+ different creators, most of whom had never written robotics code before. Last week another batch of almost 3,000 robots went out to customers worldwide, taking the install base to nearly 10,000 units in the wild. More than 1,000 additional units will ship in the next 30 days.

retrieved_docs.title: CrewAI: Role-Based Multi-Agent Orchestration
retrieved_docs.snippet:
CrewAI: Role-Based Multi-Agent Orchestration

CrewAI is an open-source framework that enables developers to build collaborative multi-agent systems where each agent has a defined role, goal, and backstory. Agents work together as a 'crew' to complete complex tasks through sequential or parallel workflows. CrewAI supports tool use, memory, and delegation, making it well-suited for automated research, content generation, and decision pipelines.

retrieved_docs.title: Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Latent Space: The Rise of Agentic AI Platforms in 2025

The Latent Space newsletter surveys the agentic AI platform landscape as of mid-2025. The analysis identifies three dominant approaches: (1) orchestration frameworks (CrewAI, AutoGen, LangGraph) focused on multi-agent coordination; (2) RAG-first platforms (LlamaIndex, Vertex AI Search) focused on grounding agents in enterprise data; and (3) evaluation-native platforms (Langfuse, Braintrust, PromptLayer) focused on reliability and measurement. The newsletter argues that evaluation will become the key differentiator as models commoditize.

retrieved_docs.title: Langfuse: Open-Source LLM Observability and Evaluation
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Langfuse: Open-Source LLM Observability and Evaluation

Langfuse is an open-source platform for tracing, evaluating, and monitoring LLM applications. It captures full traces of agent runs including prompts, completions, tool calls, and latency. Langfuse integrates with LangChain, LlamaIndex, and OpenAI SDK, and supports human annotation workflows for building evaluation datasets. It is used by teams to detect regressions, measure hallucination rates, and optimize prompt quality.

retrieved_docs.title: Tavily — about
retrieved_docs.snippet:
Tavily — about

/about us
The agentic web is vast and unstructured. Tavily delivers high-quality, fresh, and structured web context, making it easy for agents to stay grounded as they scale from prototypes to production.
/our mission
Our mission is to help AI agents navigate the web quickly and efficiently by shaping the context they see. We prioritize relevance, freshness, and efficiency so agents reason accurately without wasting tokens.
/team
We’re a team of collaborators, builders, and deep thinkers, obsessed with making reliable web access possible for AI agents in the real world. We move quickly, support each other, and stay focused on shipping systems that deliver real impact in production.

retrieved_docs.title: LangGraph: Stateful Agent Orchestration with Graphs
retrieved_docs.snippet:
LangGraph: Stateful Agent Orchestration with Graphs

LangGraph, built on top of LangChain, provides a graph-based abstraction for building reliable, stateful LLM agents. Developers define nodes (agent steps) and edges (transitions) in a directed graph, enabling cyclic workflows, human-in-the-loop checkpoints, and fine-grained state management. LangGraph is widely used for production-grade agentic pipelines requiring persistence and controllability.
